title: Program Editor Keys
description:
Program Editor Keys

Program Editor Keys

Key	Action

Home	Go to beginning of current line.

End	Go to end of current line.

Ins	Toggle insert and overtype modes.

Del	If text is selected, deletes selection without putting it in the Clipboard

. If no text is selected, deletes next character after caret.

Tab	Insert a tab character at cursor position. The tab spacing can be set with the Preferences: Editor command in the Setup Menu. If one or more lines is selected, then the Indent command will execute and move all of the selected lines to the right one tab stop.

Shift+Tab	If one or more lines is selected, then the Outdent command will execute and move all of the selected lines to the left one tab stop.

Ctrl+Home	Go to top of program

Ctrl+End	Go to bottom of program

Ctrl+A	Select entire program.

Ctrl+C or Ctrl+Ins	Execute Copy command. Copies current selection to the Clipboard

.

Ctrl+N	Insert empty line at current line.

Ctrl+S	Execute Save command. Saves the current file.

Ctrl+V or Shift+Ins	Execute Paste command. Paste from Clipboard to current selection.

Ctrl+X or Shift+Del	Execute Cut command. Cut current selection and put in Clipboard.

Ctrl+Shift+Y	Delete the current line and put in Clipboard.

Ctrl+Y	Redo last undo.

Ctrl+Z	Undo last change.

F1	Jump to help for keyword if cursor is in a keyword.

Shift+F2	Jump to function definition if cursor is in a function name for a Call or Xqt statement.

Shift+Enter	Temporarily disable auto line format. If you don't want a line to be formatted by the editor and the Auto Format editor preference is enabled, then type in the line, hold down a shift key, then press ENTER.
